d: Script to find detached instances: Use figma_execute with code that traverses all nodes recursively. A detached instance is a frame that was once an instance but lost its link. Detection: look for frames with names matching component naming patterns (slash-separated like Button/Primary) but node.type === 'FRAME' instead of 'INSTANCE'. Also check for frames that have no componentId but match known component layer structures. Flag these for reconnection or cleanup.

To reconnect detached instances: 1) Identify the original main component using name matching or structure comparison. 2) Create new instance of correct component using figma_instantiate_component. 3) Copy overrides (text content, fills, visibility) from detached frame to new instance. 4) Position new instance at same location. 5) Delete old detached frame. RULE: Always verify visually after reconnection — some overrides may not transfer cleanly.

Script to find components with zero instances in the file: Use figma_execute to traverse document. For each node, if node.type === 'INSTANCE', record node.mainComponent.id. After full scan, compare against all main components (node.type === 'COMPONENT'). Components with zero recorded instances are unused. CAUTION: component may be used in OTHER files via library — only flag as unused if this is the single source file, not a published library. For libraries: check library usage analytics if available.

Detection strategy for duplicate components: 1) Name similarity — components with near-identical names (Button vs Btn vs PrimaryButton). 2) Structure similarity — same number of children, same types, same nesting depth. 3) Property similarity — same variant properties with same values. 4) Visual similarity — use figma_capture_screenshot on both and compare. Merge strategy: pick the more complete component as primary, migrate all instances to it, deprecate the duplicate. RULE: Never delete immediately — deprecate first, verify, then remove after 1 sprint.

Script to find layers with default Figma names: Use figma_execute to traverse all nodes. Flag any node whose name matches default patterns: /^(Rectangle|Ellipse|Frame|Group|Line|Text|Vector|Star|Polygon|Image)\s*\d*$/. Count flagged nodes per page. Priority: rename layers inside components first (they propagate to instances), then rename in design pages. Batch rename using figma_rename_node. Naming convention: descriptive purpose nouns (Primary Button, Hero Image, Nav Container).

t: Find Hardcoded Colors Not Using Variables
d: Script to find hardcoded color values: Use figma_execute to traverse nodes and check fills/strokes. For each fill, check if it has boundVariables property. Fills WITHOUT boundVariables are hardcoded. Collect: node name, node id, page, hardcoded hex value. Group results by hex value to identify most common hardcoded colors. Suggest variable mappings: find closest existing variable by color distance (deltaE). RULE: Start with most-used hardcoded colors first — biggest impact.

t: Find Hardcoded Spacing Not Using Variables
d: Script to find hardcoded spacing: Check auto layout frames for gap, paddingTop/Right/Bottom/Left values that are not bound to variables. Also check fixed width/height values on components. Compare against spacing scale (4,8,12,16,20,24,32,40,48,64). Values not matching the scale are likely inconsistent. Group by value to find patterns. Suggest variable bindings for each.

t: Find Hardcoded Typography Not Using Styles
d: Script to find text nodes not using text styles: Use figma_execute to find all TEXT nodes. Check if node.textStyleId is set. Text without textStyleId is using hardcoded typography. Collect: font family, size, weight, line-height. Group by combination to find most common hardcoded typography. Suggest matching text style from existing styles. RULE: Fix typography in components first — instances inherit.

Script to audit corner radius values across file: Use figma_execute to traverse all frames and rectangles. Collect cornerRadius values. Group by value. Flag values not in the design system radius scale (0,2,4,8,12,16,20,24,28,9999). Identify nodes using non-standard radii. Check if radius values are bound to variables. Suggest corrections to nearest standard value.

Script to audit shadow effects: Use figma_execute to find all nodes with effects array. For each dropShadow effect, collect offset (x,y), blur, spread, color. Group by combination. Compare against elevation scale defined in design system. Flag non-standard shadow combinations. Check if effects use effect styles. Suggest style bindings.

Check for component sets with too many variants: Use figma_search_components to list all component sets. For each set, count variants. Flag sets with >30 variants — likely candidate for restructuring. Analysis: check if variants could be replaced with component properties (boolean toggle instead of variant), variables (color mode instead of color variant), or nested instance swaps (icon variant → instance swap property). Recommend restructuring strategy per flagged set.

Check for excessively deep component nesting: Use figma_execute to traverse component trees. Count nesting depth (instance inside instance inside instance). Flag components with nesting depth > 5 — may cause performance issues and override complexity. Recommend flattening opportunities: replace deeply nested structures with simpler compositions. RULE: Max recommended nesting depth is 4-5 levels.

Check how many components use auto layout: Use figma_execute to scan all COMPONENT nodes. Check if each has layoutMode set (HORIZONTAL, VERTICAL, or grid). Components WITHOUT auto layout are likely using absolute positioning — less flexible, harder to maintain. Target: 95%+ of components should use auto layout. Flag non-auto-layout components for refactoring.

Comprehensive variable usage audit: 1) Count total unique colors used in file vs colors bound to variables. 2) Count total spacing values vs variable-bound spacing. 3) Count total border-radius values vs variable-bound. 4) Calculate coverage percentage per category. Target: >90% variable coverage for mature design systems. Tools: figma_get_variables for defined vars, figma_execute for scanning usage. Report: coverage %, top 10 unbounded values per category.

Check variable collection health: 1) Are primitives, semantics, and component tokens in separate collections? 2) Are variables properly scoped (colors not appearing in spacing pickers)? 3) Are naming conventions consistent (all lowercase, slash-separated, no mixed formats)? 4) Are modes properly set up (Light/Dark at minimum)? 5) Are there orphaned variables (defined but never used)? Use figma_get_variables with format='summary' then detailed checks.

Standard audit report structure: 1) HEALTH SCORE (0-100): weighted average of all audit dimensions. 2) CRITICAL (must fix): detached instances in components, hardcoded colors in published components, missing text styles. 3) WARNING (should fix): unnamed layers, inconsistent radii, excessive variants. 4) INFO (nice to fix): suboptimal nesting, unused local components, non-standard spacing. 5) METRICS: total components, total variants, variable coverage %, style coverage %, named layer %, auto layout %. 6) RECOMMENDATIONS: prioritized action list with effort estimates.

Design system health score formula: Variable Coverage (25%): % of color/spacing/radius values using variables. Style Coverage (20%): % of text nodes using text styles. Named Layers (15%): % of layers with non-default names (in components). Auto Layout Usage (15%): % of components using auto layout. Component Integrity (15%): % of instances not detached. Consistency (10%): % of values matching defined scales. Score 90+: Excellent, 70-89: Good, 50-69: Needs attention, <50: Critical debt.

Steps to merge duplicate components: 1) Identify candidates via structure comparison. 2) Choose primary (most instances, better structured, more complete properties). 3) Document all overrides used on secondary component instances. 4) Add missing properties/variants to primary to cover secondary's use cases. 5) Swap all secondary instances to primary using figma_execute (find instances, swap main component). 6) Verify overrides transfer. 7) Move secondary to deprecated page. 8) Delete after verification period (1-2 sprints). RULE: Never merge across different functional categories.

Strategies to reduce component variant explosion: 1) BOOLEAN PROPERTIES: replace Show/Hide variants with boolean toggle (e.g., hasIcon=true/false instead of With-Icon/Without-Icon variants). 2) VARIABLES: replace color variants with variable modes (Light/Dark instead of Button-Light/Button-Dark). 3) INSTANCE SWAP: replace icon variants with instance swap property (swap any icon instead of variant per icon). 4) NESTED COMPONENTS: extract varying parts into nested sub-components. Result: 18 variants → 3 variants + 3 properties is common. RULE: Each variant should represent a genuinely different LAYOUT, not just a different value.

Steps to reduce component nesting depth: 1) Identify deepest nesting chains. 2) Check if intermediate wrapper components add value (properties, states). 3) If wrapper only passes through without adding logic — merge into parent. 4) Replace nested instances with direct child elements where the nested component has no reuse outside this parent. 5) Use slots (2025) to allow flexible content without deep nesting. Target: max 4 levels deep. Example: Page > Card > CardHeader > HeaderActions > IconButton — consider merging CardHeader actions directly into Card.

Strategy to migrate from color styles to variables: 1) Export all color styles with figma_get_styles. 2) Create matching variable structure (primitives + semantics). 3) Use figma_setup_design_tokens to create variables with same values. 4) Use figma_execute to scan all nodes using color styles, rebind to matching variables. 5) Verify visually with figma_capture_screenshot. 6) Keep styles for gradients and composite effects (variables don't support these). 7) Remove migrated color styles after verification. RULE: Migrate in stages — one collection at a time.

Audit and add missing component properties: 1) Scan components for text layers without text properties — add text property for each editable text. 2) Scan for nested instances without instance swap properties — add swap property for customizable nested components. 3) Scan for toggleable layers without boolean properties — add boolean for layers that are sometimes hidden in instances. 4) Check if variant properties cover all needed states (default, hover, active, disabled, focus). Use figma_add_component_property programmatically. RULE: Expose only what designers need — don't over-expose internal layers.

Checklist before publishing library update: 1) All components use auto layout. 2) All colors/spacing/radii use variables (no hardcoded). 3) All text uses text styles. 4) All layers named descriptively (no Frame 1, Rectangle 3). 5) All components have description. 6) All variant properties are consistent across sets. 7) All interactive states included (default/hover/active/disabled/focus). 8) Tested in Light and Dark mode. 9) Touch targets meet minimum (44pt iOS / 48dp Android). 10) No detached instances inside components. 11) Component documentation updated. 12) Changelog entry written.

Recommended audit schedule: WEEKLY: Quick check — new components follow naming conventions, no detached instances in active work. MONTHLY: Variable coverage check, unused component scan, design debt backlog review. QUARTERLY: Full audit — health score calculation, deep structure analysis, variant optimization review, token alignment with code. PER-RELEASE: Pre-publish checklist, visual regression check, breaking change documentation. Assign audit tasks to design system maintainer role.

Template for figma_execute scan scripts: function scanAll(node, callback, depth=0) { callback(node, depth); if ('children' in node) { for (const child of node.children) { scanAll(child, callback, depth+1); } } } const results = []; scanAll(figma.root, (node, depth) => { /* your check here */ }); return { total: results.length, items: results.slice(0, 50) }; RULE: Always slice results to prevent timeout — Figma plugin execution has 30s limit. Process in batches for large files.
